result:
All changes are complete and verified. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Root Cause

All four naming rules used ASCII-only character classes in their regex patterns (`[a-z]`, `[A-Z]`, `[a-zA-Z]`), which excluded valid Unicode letters like Nordic characters (å, ä, ö, æ, ø).

## Changes Made

**4 files modified**, replacing ASCII character classes with Unicode property classes:

1. **`PackageNameRule.kt`** (`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/.../PackageNameRule.kt:36`):
   - `[a-z_]` → `[\\p{Ll}_]` and `[a-zA-Z\\d_]` → `[\\p{L}\\d_]`

2. **`ClassNamingRule.kt`** (`ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/.../ClassNamingRule.kt:54`):
   - `[A-Z]` → `[\\p{Lu}]` and `[A-Za-z\\d]` → `[\\p{L}\\d]`

3. **`FunctionNamingRule.kt`** (`ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/.../FunctionNamingRule.kt:71-72`):
   - `[a-z]` → `[\\p{Ll}]` and `[A-Za-z\\d]` → `[\\p{L}\\d]` (both regular and test function patterns)

4. **`PropertyNamingRule.kt`** (`ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/.../PropertyNamingRule.kt:139-141`):
   - Lower camel case: `[a-z]` → `[\\p{Ll}]`, `[a-zA-Z0-9]` → `[\\p{L}0-9]`
   - Screaming snake case: `[A-Z]` → `[\\p{Lu}]`, `[_A-Z0-9]` → `[_\\p{Lu}0-9]`
   - Backing property: `[a-z]` → `[\\p{Ll}]`, `[a-zA-Z0-9]` → `[\\p{L}0-9]`

The Unicode property classes used:
- `\p{Ll}` — any Unicode lowercase letter (includes å, ä, ö, æ, ø, etc.)
- `\p{Lu}` — any Unicode uppercase letter (includes Å, Ä, Ö, Æ, Ø, etc.)
- `\p{L}` — any Unicode letter (both cases)
